pageSubtitle: Overview of your RabbitMQ server health and performance
addServerDescription: Add a RabbitMQ server connection to start monitoring your queues and messages.
selectServerPrompt: Please select a RabbitMQ server to view the dashboard
chooseServerFromSidebar: Choose a RabbitMQ server from the sidebar to view its dashboard.
resourceUsageSubtitle: Cluster-wide resource metrics and system usage
connectedNodesSubtitle: RabbitMQ cluster node status and metrics
upgradeRequired: Upgrade to Developer or Enterprise plan to view detailed alert information and configure alert thresholds.
serverLimitReached: You've reached your server limit. Upgrade to add more servers.
addServerFormDescription: Connect to your RabbitMQ server via the Management API. Ensure the Management plugin is enabled and use its port (default: 15672).
editServerFormDescription: Update the connection details for your RabbitMQ server.
defPublish: Rate at which messages are entering the server
defDeliver: Rate at which messages are delivered to consumers
defAck: Rate at which message acknowledgments are received
defDeliverGet: Combined rate of deliver + get operations
defDeliverNoAck: Rate at which messages are delivered without requiring acknowledgment
defConfirm: Rate at which publisher confirms are received
defGet: Rate at which messages are retrieved via basic.get
defGetNoAck: Rate at which messages are retrieved without acknowledgment
defGetEmpty: Rate at which basic.get calls return empty
defRedeliver: Rate at which messages are redelivered
defReject: Rate at which messages are rejected by consumers
defReturnUnroutable: Rate at which unroutable messages are returned
defDropUnroutable: Rate at which unroutable messages are silently dropped
defDiskReads: Rate at which messages are read from disk
defDiskWrites: Rate at which messages are written to disk
basicRatesAlert: Only instantaneous rates are available. To enable historical time-series charts, set management.rates_mode = detailed in your RabbitMQ configuration and restart the node.
defTotal: Total number of messages in all queues
defReady: Messages ready to be delivered to consumers
defUnacked: Messages delivered but not yet acknowledged
queuedMetricsNote: These metrics show the current state of messages across all queues in the server.
connectionTooltip: Total active client connections to the RabbitMQ cluster
channelTooltip: Total AMQP channels across all connections
consumerTooltip: Total active message consumers across all queues
exchangeTooltip: Total exchanges configured in the cluster
serverUrlLabel: Paste your RabbitMQ Management API URL or AMQP(S) URL
serverUrlHelp: We'll auto-fill the fields below. You can also enter details manually.
urlParsedNoCreds: URL parsed — enter your credentials below to test
urlParseError: Couldn't parse this URL. Check the format or enter details manually.
serverNameHint: You can change this later. Other team members will see this name.
useHttpsHint: Enable for secure connections (CloudAMQP, AWS, etc.)
virtualHostHint: Selected by default when you open this server.
tunnelLocalhostDescription: To monitor a RabbitMQ server running on localhost (development only), expose it using a tunnel service.
tunnelDetectedDescription: Your tunnel URL has been detected. HTTPS will be automatically enabled. Make sure your tunnel is running and accessible.
tunnelOption1Help: Install ngrok first at <1>ngrok.com/download</1>. Use the HTTPS URL in the Host field.
tunnelOption2Help: Copy the HTTPS URL provided (e.g. https://abc123.loca.lt) and paste it in the Host field above. Port will default to 443.
unsupportedVersionTitle: This version isn't supported on your plan
unsupportedVersionDescription: The server reports RabbitMQ {{version}}. Upgrade your plan to connect to this version.
versionSupportFreeHint: Upgrade to Developer or Enterprise to connect to any version from 3.0 to 4.x, including non-LTS releases.
versionSupportUnsupportedHint: Connecting to an unsupported version will be rejected.
managementPluginRequiredHint: Make sure the RabbitMQ Management plugin is enabled. The Management API port (default 15672) is different from the AMQP port (5672).
